---
title: "Hands-on Exercise 4a"
author: "JIN QINHAO"
---

## **Getting Started**

### **Installing and loading the packages**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(ggdist, ggridges, ggthemes,
               colorspace, tidyverse)
```
:::


### **Data import**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Rows: 322 Columns: 7
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (4): ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE
dbl (3): ENGLISH, MATHS, SCIENCE

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```


:::
:::


## **Visualising Distribution with Ridgeline Plot**

### **Plotting ridgeline graph: ggridges method**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    bandwidth = 3.4,
    fill = lighten("#7097BB", .3),
    color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL, expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))) +
  theme_ridges()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Varying fill colors along the x axis**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS,
           fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Temp. [F]",
                       option = "C") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL, expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))) +
  theme_ridges()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Mapping the probabilities directly onto colour**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = 0.5 - abs(0.5-stat(ecdf)))) +
  stat_density_ridges(geom = "density_ridges_gradient", 
                      calc_ecdf = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Tail probability",
                       direction = -1) +
  theme_ridges()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning: `stat(ecdf)` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `after_stat(ecdf)` instead.
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Ridgeline plots with quantile lines**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE, 
    quantiles = 4,
    quantile_lines = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Quartiles") +
  theme_ridges()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE, 
    quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975)
    ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Probability",
    values = c("#FF0000A0", "#A0A0A0A0", "#0000FFA0"),
    labels = c("(0, 0.025]", "(0.025, 0.975]", "(0.975, 1]")
  ) +
  theme_ridges()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Picking joint bandwidth of 3.18
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## **Visualising Distribution with Raincloud Plot**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Plotting a Half Eye graph**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### **Adding the boxplot with `geom_boxplot()`Coordinated Multiple Views with ggiraph**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_dots(side = "left", 
            justification = 1.2, 
            binwidth = .5,
            dotsize = 2)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## **Finishing touch**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
  ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_dots(side = "left", 
            justification = 1.2, 
            binwidth = .5,
            overflow = "keep",
            dotsize = 1.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_economist()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex04a_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::
